text: Last class you resolved a dispute with mediation and arbitration.
Today the final draft is ready, and the contract is read before it is signed.

Thank you for the final draft. I see that the Goods are construed as the products that are delivered. The definitions are clear.
Good. And all the schedules are incorporated into the agreement, so they are read as one document. The price annex is attached.
I also see that the old letter of intent is superseded by this agreement. The payment terms are stipulated in the annex.
Exactly. Any later change is deemed accepted only when it is signed by both of us. A missing signature can render the change invalid.
Understood. So the contract is executed when it is signed by both parties. The draft is ready, and I have no more comments.
Perfect. The final version is sent to both teams today, and the agreement is signed on Friday. Thank you for the careful review.
The word Goods is construed as the products delivered.
The contract is executed when it is signed.

Hi Simone, this is Daniel from the legal team. The service agreement is almost ready, and the final draft is sent to you now. Before it is signed, please review three points. First, in the definitions, the word Goods is defined as the products that are delivered under this contract, and the word Services is construed in the same way. Second, all the schedules are incorporated into the agreement, so they are read as one document. Please confirm that the price annex is attached, because it is referred to in clause five. Third, the old letter of intent is superseded by this agreement, and the change is deemed accepted when both parties sign. The payment terms are stipulated in clause eight, and notice is given in writing. If anything is not clear, it will be discussed on Thursday before the contract is executed. Thank you, and talk soon.

First, the final draft of the service agreement is sent to Simone for review. She checks the definitions and sees that the Goods are defined as the products that are delivered under the contract. Next, she confirms that all the schedules are incorporated and the price annex is attached. Then she notes that the old letter of intent is superseded by the new agreement. Finally, both parties sign, and the contract is executed.
